channel.title: C/C++ on yexca'Blog
channel.description: Recent content in C/C++ on yexca'Blog

item.title: Setting Up C Environment in VS Code
item.description:
📢 This article was translated by gemini-3-flash-preview

This article was written by Hiyoung

Original article: https://blog.hiyoung.icu/2022/12/15/997871bc263d/

VS Code is just a plain text editor, not an IDE (Integrated Development Environment). It doesn’t include a compiler or many other features, so you need to install the compiler yourself.

Step 1: Download the software from the VS Code official website: Visual Studio Code – Code Editing. Redefined

Step 2: We need to download a compiler. For C, we use GCC. Link: MinGW-w64 – for 32 and 64 bit Windows download | SourceForge.net . Select x86_64-posix-seh from the latest version. The website download might be slow, so here is a Baidu Netdisk link provided below.
